mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding real output capacity, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the subsequent month as production momentum persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO direction over 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year walk-forward window, or the proportion of positive returns after INDPRO increases does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending power and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Following UNRATE crossings above 5.5%, the median 60-day forward return is positive, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales reflect accelerating consumer and residential demand, which jointly drive corporate profits and stock prices higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS are rising month-over-month, the 20-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the joint signal has no significant predictive power (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (federal funds rate below 5%), accommodative monetary policy supports equity valuations through lower discount rates and cheaper borrowing, generating positive forward returns over the short term.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 5%, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 55% of periods, or the rolling 2-year IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth to less than 1.5% year-over-year signals an approaching economic slowdown, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: After GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, the median 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the negative predictive accuracy is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the Consumer Price Index signal rising inflation, which erodes real earnings and forces the central bank to tighten, producing negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, the 5-day forward return is positive more than 55% of the time, or the rank IC is below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in nonfarm payrolls combined with stagnant industrial output indicates labor market tightness without corresponding productivity gains, raising unit labor costs and compressing margins, leading to negative near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises faster than INDPRO over a month, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the joint signal's IC is below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment environment (UNRATE below 6%), robust retail sales growth reflects strong consumer confidence and spending, which boosts corporate revenues and stock prices over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6%, rising RSAFS leads to median negative 5-day forward returns, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy is below 55%.
